rulings.Case Title: EST. OF FRANK W. HEGGBLADE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Terminate Testamentary Trust; Waiver Of Accounting; Release Of Trustee (Probate Code Section 15403)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/5/2023. Please provide the following material seven days prior to the hearing: 1. Need notice of hearing to be given to the court appointed personal representative (if any) of deceased beneficiary, Virginia Baker, or, if no personal represented has been appointed, all persons who would succeed Virginia Baker's interest, including but not limited her heirs at law. 2. Verified supplemental to the petition to set forth who are entitled to the notice of hearing mentioned in #1. 3. Authority under the court can grant the trust to be terminated as the parties agreed when the beneficiary deceased prior to the termination. Supplement filed 3/8/2023, stating that the parties believe the agreement is binding; however, it does not provide ground for the belief. 4. Need a copy of the trust or extract of a provision that may govern distribution in case of one of the beneficiaries deceased. 5. Proposed Order needs to be submitted.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF KRISTINA MOLLOY MILLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] In hopes of not continuing the hearing, the pleading was reviewed briefly online. (2) The petition is not verified. (3) Assets on hand at the end of the first account included $13,360.46 in the "Guardian's Savings Account" but this accounting begins with just the funds in the SF Fire Credit Union CD. (4) No original bank statement appears to have been filed yet to verify the balance in the Chase account as of 9/30/21. (5) The order allowing the second amended first account ordered the guardian to address the $6,600 she paid herself retroactively during that first accounting period. (6) The order dated 8/7/18 appointed the temporary guardian but did not mention anything about splitting monthly pension payments. The order appointing the guardian dated 9/18/18 authorized the guardian to expend up to $1,100 in guardianship assets for the support, care, and maintenance of the minor, plus Social Security survivor benefits in the amount of $1,941. Why are those disbursements for the minor's care not reflected in the accounting? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: * ERROR *
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/05/2023, pending a disposition of the related heirship petition. The following issues have not been addressed since the initial hearing on 12/12/2022: 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) No original statements of the bank accounts listed on Schedule F have been filed. See LRSF 14.34(D). 4) A copy of the petition must be served on the person who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. See Probate Code section 1252(a). 5) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 6) Please file a copy of the closing escrow statement for the sale of the estate real property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD J. HUNDGEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/22/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (2) Notice must also include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Paragraph 8 of the petition does not state when the other heir waived her right to an accounting. No waiver of accounting was attached to the petition. The register of actions does not reflect any filed waivers of accounting. (4) No allowance of the creditor's claim appears to have been filed yet. [Probate Code Section 9250(b).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/12/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (An unsigned order is in itself a proposed order; there is no need to put "proposed" in the caption.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES M. ARTHURS TRUST DATED OCTOBER 9, 2015
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Confirmation Of Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The issue was not whether the predeceased son "left children of his own" but whether or not that son was survived by ANY ISSUE. The petitioner must verify this, not merely rely on provisions in an unadmitted will. Furthermore, the will attached to the petition states that the testator had "no other deceased children or grandchildren," which also does not address whether the precdeceased son was survived by issue, which is not limited to just children and grandchildren. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order 1)Removing Trustee & Surcharge 2)Compelling Trustee To Account 3)Reducing Trustee'S Use Of Trust Funds For Litigation And 5)Awarding Damages And Attorneys' Fee And Costs For Financial Elder Abuse
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/23. (No earlier availability.) An UN-endorsed filed copy of objections to the underlying petition was received by the assigned examiner on 4/3/23. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of Notice for a trust petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the face of which (item 1) the full and correct title of the petition must be set forth, and on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) It does not appear that original statements have been filed to verify the assets on hand. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) The accounting begins with values that are not supported. How were they determined? (5) For what services were legal fees paid to various firms? They may need to be supported. (6) It is unclear how the trustee's management fees were calculated and why there was a lump sum for 2018 and a significant increase in 2020. (7) Reimbursements to an individual, particularly a fiduciary, must show who the original payee was. (8) Why are there public storage fees? The accounting does not reflect any tangible personal property. (9) For what loan was Eclipse Property Management paid $77,242.30 on 6/18/19? (10) An objection to the accounting was filed on 4/6/23. No courtesy copy has been received yet by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] The additional attached property management summaries are not in a court-conformed accounting format and have not been reviewed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Only one courtesy copy needs to be provided to the court. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANNA PAYNE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For 1. Invalid Donative Transfer Pursuant To Probate Code 21360 Et Seq, 2. Disinheritance Of An Heir Under California Probate Code
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/5/2023. Please supplment Petition and Objection to clear procedural issues, respectively, as follows: Procedural Issues on Petition: 1. Need a copy of all instruments subject to this petition. Petitioners allege that they are interested parties; however, no supporting documents have been provided. 2. Proof of service of Summons on Catherine Neto. 3. A copy of Power of Attorney under which Mr. Steven Jackson's can petition on behalf of Ms. Jackson. 4. Petition makes multiple claims under various code sections. Each claim needs to provide proper notice of hearing to all persons who are entitled to notice under specific provisions. Petition needs to set forth relevant information. 5. Proposed Order must be submitted. Procedural Issues on Objection: 1. Need a copy of the instruments subject to this objection. 2. Objection/Response to set forth who are entitled to receive a copy of Objection/Response and corresponding Proof of service to all the persons must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRUCE EDWARD BENHAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/5/23. (No earlier available date.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) It appears, from a cursory glance at the first page of the petition, that not all items of the petition were completed. (4) The only correct form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If an interested party has directed in writing that notice be mailed only to his or her attorney, that written direction must be filed. Deadline is 5/22/23 for delivering the proposed order and courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
